read_targets.notes: SQL Server-specific, to be replaced with auto-increment or UUID

reference_data.notes: Reference data could be managed via admin interface (not in current scope)

external_storage.cleanup_strategy: orphaned images not cleaned up (technical debt)

transaction_boundaries.notes: DB operation is atomic, but image operation is separate (risk of inconsistency)
Image update happens BEFORE DB update (risk of orphaned images if DB fails)
Image is not deleted from storage (technical debt)
Read-only operation with navigation property loading

strong_consistency: Catalog table writes (must be immediately visible to reads)
Foreign key constraints (CatalogBrandId, CatalogTypeId must exist)
eventual_consistency_acceptable: Image storage (delay between upload and visibility is acceptable)
compensating_actions_needed: Cleanup orphaned product images after delete (not implemented)

hilo_sequence.details: Use auto-increment PRIMARY KEY or UUID instead of HiLo
hilo_sequence.data_migration: Export with existing IDs, use IDENTITY_INSERT or map to new sequence

image_storage.details: Use S3-compatible storage (MinIO, AWS S3) or local filesystem
image_storage.data_migration: Copy all blobs from Azure to new storage, update PictureUri computation

transaction_management.details: Use SQLAlchemy transaction scope, consider saga pattern for image operations
transaction_management.improvement: Wrap image + DB operations in compensating transaction or make image operations idempotent

notes: All data access is explicit and traceable through CatalogService
No shared write conflicts (single seam application)
Reference data (CatalogType, CatalogBrand) is read-only from application perspective
HiLo sequence is SQL Server-specific and adds complexity (to be replaced)
Image operations are not in DB transaction scope (potential inconsistency)
No cleanup of orphaned images (temp images or deleted product images)
All DB operations are synchronous (Entity Framework 6, no async/await)
